pi_items.source: Found in task.json description, setup_task.sh target paths, and install_wireshark.sh download URLs
Found in task.json description, setup_task.sh initialization, and export_result.sh evaluation logic
The task instructions explicitly require packet analysis of HTTP network traffic to reconstruct session data

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on a real, non-synthetic PCAP file (`http.cap`) downloaded from the official Wireshark sample captures wiki. Analysis of the PCAP via `tshark` reveals 2 total HTTP requests: one to `/download.html` (destination IP 65.208.228.223) and another to `/pagead/ads...` (destination IP 216.239.59.99). The expected report output path explicitly checked by the evaluation script is `http_analysis_report.txt`. The evaluation dynamically extracts ground truth targets, but has a notable flaw: it identifies the single expected web server IP as `216.239.59.99` by sorting the destination IPs alphabetically, missing the primary web server `65.208.228.223`. The ground truth HTTP request count is exactly 2, the status code is 200, and the User-Agent begins with 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows; U; Windows NT 5.1; en-US; rv:1.6) Gecko/20040113'.
